decisions.title: Adopt luhtech-schema as schema-first execution substrate
decisions.context: Eight months of platform construction at LuhTech Holdings produced infrastructure, schemas, ventures, and AI agents in parallel. Hand-maintained hygiene cannot scale: drift accumulates faster than a solo founder can manually defend against. The portfolio needs mechanical validation as the durable answer to the hygiene problem.
decisions.decision: Adopt the luhtech-schema Python tool as the single substrate for all schema-touching operations across the portfolio. Schemas are not documentation; they are the executable specification of the platform. Every schema-touching commit flows through canonical contracts validated by luhtech-schema, integrated into pre-commit hooks, CI gates, and MCP exposure to AI agents.
decisions.rationale: The tool unifies and supersedes the eight planning-arc decisions D1 through D8 captured in LUHTECH-PLATFORM-SCHEMA-STRATEGY-AND-TOOLING-2026-05-17. Building in-house rather than adopting a third-party linter is justified by portfolio-specific canonical rules (URL $id host enforcement, acronym catalog enforcement, classification and boundary semantics) that have no off-the-shelf equivalent. The T0–T9 build sequence provides a concrete incremental path from scaffold to full enforcement.

positive: All schemas in both registries migrate to URL $id form at https://schemas.luh.tech/ — D2, closes SH-1 at T5.
Every schema carries an explicit top-level semver version field — D6, closes SH-2 at T3.
Acronyms catalog becomes a schema-validated artifact governed by acronyms-catalog.schema.json — D4, closes SH-7 at T1.
Schema-registry rename luh-tech/luh-tech-roadmap-template to luh-tech/schema-registry executed 2026-05-18 — D3.
venture-summary.json and metrics-pipeline.json deprecated from active dataflows; schemas retained as canonical contracts — D7.
negative: Migration burden: T3 and T5 require touching every schema file in the registry to add version fields and migrate URN $id to URL form.
risks: CI enforcement gate (T8) must not land before all consuming repos have been updated — otherwise PRs block.

alternatives.rejectionReason: Scaling failure mode already observed: drift accumulates faster than a solo founder can manually defend against.

alternatives.option: Adopt an existing third-party schema linter
alternatives.rejectionReason: Portfolio-specific rules (URL $id host enforcement, acronym catalog enforcement, classification and boundary semantics) require plugin development equivalent in scope to the in-house tool.

externalLinks.description: Locked strategy document — captures D1 through D8, T0 through T9 build sequence, SH-1 through SH-7 hygiene gate.

decisions.title: luhtech-schema T1: acronyms catalog as schema-validated artifact (SH-7) and D9 streams principle
decisions.context: T0 closed with the luhtech-schema scaffold. T1 lands the first canonical instance — the LuhTech vocabulary catalog — parsed from canonical_glossary_v1.2 (2026-05-05, 162 terms, 16 sections). The act of landing this instance surfaces D9: all canonical artifacts should be served via stream URLs rather than file paths.
decisions.decision: Adopt the acronyms catalog as the first portfolio-canonical instance at stream URL https://schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.json. Adopt D9 as portfolio-wide architectural commitment. Add acronyms check and acronyms register subcommands to luhtech-schema. Schema at https://schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.schema.json.
decisions.rationale: D9 generalizes D2 (URL $id for schemas) to all canonical artifacts. The glossary v1.2 is patent-prep quality and the right seed for a schema-validated catalog. Building the acronyms subcommands alongside the catalog closes SH-7 and validates D9 end-to-end at T1.

positive: D9 LOCKED: every future canonical instance gets a stream URL; file references are tech debt.
SH-7 CLOSED: acronyms catalog is schema-validated; canonical vocabulary has a contract.
luhtech-schema validate accepts http(s) URLs (D9-aware).
acronyms check fetches from stream URL by default; --local for offline dev / pre-commit hooks.
162 terms parsed from glossary v1.2 across 16 sections (119 LOCKED, 32 PATENT-OWNED, 11 DEPRECATED).
negative: Catalog instance requires schema-validated updates; ad-hoc edits must conform to acronyms-catalog.schema.json.
risks: Stream URL (schemas.luh.tech/acronyms-catalog.json) must be live before downstream consumers use acronyms check without --local.

alternatives.option: Catalog as repo-local file with path references
alternatives.rejectionReason: Tech debt per D9; cross-repo consumers couple to producer file location.

alternatives.option: Use existing ACRONYMS-CATALOG-2026-05-04.json (215 terms, different schema shape)
alternatives.rejectionReason: Different term shape (type/category/domain/verified vs status/sourceStatus) and carries deprecated luhtech.dev $schema URL. Migration to schemas.luh.tech is SH-1/T5 scope. Glossary v1.2 is the correct seed for the new canonical schema shape.

externalLinks.description: Schema governing the canonical vocabulary catalog.
D9 stream URL for the catalog instance.

decisions.title: luhtech-schema T2: classify + map + local resolver + 403 fix
decisions.context: T1 closed with D9 stream-fetch in production but surfaced two bugs: (1) Python urllib gets 403 from Cloudflare with its default user-agent — acronyms check silently failed without --local. (2) jsonschema auto-fetches $refs over the network, triggering a deprecation warning. Substrate also confirmed 42/42 D2-compliant schemas and 41/42 SH-2 violations — the planned T3 target. No existing classification metadata in any schema; directory structure IS the natural taxonomy.
decisions.decision: Ship T2 as four interlocked deliverables: (1) fix 403 by adding explicit User-Agent header to all urllib calls; (2) wire referencing.Registry built from schemas/_meta/schema-map.json to retire auto-fetch warning; (3) classify + map subcommands producing schemas/_meta/schema-map.json — second D9 stream artifact; (4) seed schema-registry .roadmap/follow-up-register.json with FU-CATALOG-STREAM-MIGRATION-1. Classification is non-invasive (external index, schemas unmodified), pragmatic 8-value layer enum.
decisions.rationale: The 403 fix and local resolver are prerequisite correctness fixes for D9. The schema-map closes the T2 milestone and becomes the substrate for T3 (add-versions sweep knows exactly which 41 schemas need version fields). Non-invasive classification respects the schemas-as-substrate principle: metadata about schemas lives in indices, not in schemas themselves.

positive: D9 stream-fetch works from Python (403 fixed for validate URL and acronyms check).
schema-map.json is second D9 stream artifact — 42-schema registry queryable from one URL.
Local $ref resolution active via referencing.Registry — no network fetch for $refs.
Tool version 0.1.0 → 0.2.0 (classify + map public surface).
schema-registry .roadmap/follow-up-register.json seeded — parity with decision-log.json.
42/42 D2-compliant, 1/42 D6-compliant — T3 scope confirmed programmatically.
negative: schema-map.json must be regenerated whenever schemas are added/renamed/removed.
risks: Ectropy domain registry (ectropy-ai/schemas) deferred from map scope — two local clones, canonical path unresolved. Filed as context for T2 DEC; resolution needed before T6.

alternatives.option: Add tier/layer fields to each schema file
alternatives.rejectionReason: Invasive; couples T2 to SH-2 migration; metadata about schemas belongs in indices not schemas.

alternatives.option: Ship 403 fix as separate PR before new T2 surface
alternatives.rejectionReason: Interlocked: map cmd validates schema-map.json via local resolver via fixed urlopen; single PR is cleaner.

externalLinks.description: D9 stream URL for the schema map instance.

decisions.title: luhtech-schema T3: migrate add-versions closes SH-2 (42/42 D6 compliance)
decisions.context: T2 closed with schema-map showing 1/42 D6 compliance. T3 substrate confirmed 41 SH-2 schemas, all rule-3 (1.0.0 default) — zero v-suffix ambiguity. Substrate also found FU-RESOLVER-PROPAGATION-1 already resolved by T2 schema-map coverage; the T2 Phase 1 warning was a substrate-ordering artifact.
decisions.decision: Ship migrate add-versions as the first mutating tool surface. Adds "version": "1.0.0" to all 41 SH-2 schemas atomically (dry-run default, --apply explicit, idempotent, atomic per-file write, order-preserving). Regenerate schema-map post-migration: d6Compliant flips 1/42 → 42/42. Close FU-RESOLVER-PROPAGATION-1 as resolved-without-code-change with substrate-ordering root cause documented.
decisions.rationale: All 41 schemas fell under rule-3 (default 1.0.0) — no v-suffix in any $id or filename, so no per-schema version negotiation needed. The migrate harness is intentionally thin at T3 and designed to extend for T4 (xaas-to-snx) and T5 (urn-to-url) without architectural changes.

positive: SH-2 CLOSED — 42/42 D6 compliance (42/42 D2 already). Registry fully D-rule compliant.
First mutating subcommand ships: luhtech-schema migrate add-versions.
Migrate framework discipline established: dry-run default, --apply explicit, idempotent, atomic per-file.
Tool version 0.2.0 → 0.3.0.
schema-map d6Compliant flips to 42/42; serves updated state at https://schemas.luh.tech/_meta/schema-map.json.
FU-RESOLVER-PROPAGATION-1 closed; substrate-ordering lesson documented.
negative: 41 schema files now carry version: 1.0.0 — any consumer that validates these schemas against a strict $schema ref will see the new field.
risks: Version 1.0.0 for all schemas implies no prior history. This is correct (no semantic versioning was tracked pre-T3). Future schema changes should increment version per semver discipline.

alternatives.rejectionReason: Over-engineered for T3; all schemas start at 1.0.0 cleanly.

decisions.title: luhtech-schema T4: schema-map L11 expansion + scope reshape for XaaS/deprecation work
decisions.context: T4's strategy-doc framing was 'migrate xaas-to-snx + v1-to-v3-deprecate'. Substrate revealed: (1) service-exchange.enum.json already encodes the canonical SNx→XaaS-supersedes mapping — code-level migration done; (2) XaaS survives in three Ectropy-domain surfaces more coherent to bundle into T5 (urn-to-url); (3) v-suffix deprecation has unresolved policy questions. Schema-map L11 expansion was a clean, concrete need.
decisions.decision: Reshape T4 to schema-map L11 expansion only. luhtech-schema map gains --include-ectropy-domain flag. classify.py adds 'domain' layer for L11 entries. schema-map.schema.json adds 'domain' to layer enum + byRegistry to meta (bump 1.0.0→1.1.0). Three FUs filed: FU-ECTROPY-XAAS-MIGRATION-1 (bundle XaaS into T5), FU-V1-TO-V3-DEPRECATE-1 (deprecation policy), FU-CATALOG-DAAS-INVESTIGATE-1 (DaaS reference investigation).
decisions.rationale: Schema-map L11 expansion has no policy dependencies and delivers immediate value: 99-schema portfolio-spanning view, L11 SH-1 baseline visible (57/57 URN), L11 D6 baseline visible (3/57 have version). Bundling XaaS into T5 avoids intermediate inconsistent states where directory paths rename but URN segments don't yet match. Deprecation policy must be explicit before mutation to avoid long-lived precedent mistakes.

positive: schema-map.json indexes 99 schemas (42 portfolio + 57 Ectropy domain) — first portfolio-spanning view.
L11 SH-1 baseline visible: 57/57 URN $id (all T5 migration targets).
L11 D6 baseline visible: 3/57 Ectropy schemas with version; 54 are SH-2 scope in the L11 registry.
Tool version 0.3.0 → 0.4.0 (multi-root capability).
schema-map.schema.json 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 (new optional fields, backward-compatible).
FU register grows to 5 entries (4 open, 1 closed) — explicit capture of deferred scope.
negative: T5 now inherits expanded scope: urn-to-url (57 URNs) + XaaS terminology cleanup (directory rename + URN segments + description strings).
risks: SNx directory naming form (csx/ vs corsx/ vs full noun) decision needed before T5 substrate — affects URN segment updates and product code imports.

alternatives.option: Ship migrate xaas-to-snx as a substantive T4 subcommand
alternatives.rejectionReason: At code/enum level the migration is already done (service-exchange.enum.json). Description-string polish alone doesn't merit a migrate subcommand.

alternatives.option: Ship migrate v1-to-v3-deprecate without policy resolution
alternatives.rejectionReason: Deprecation marker form is a long-lived precedent; per-family active-variant identification needs verification (luhtech-enums v2 is the active one, not the unversioned stub — substrate inverted naive assumption).

externalLinks.description: Post-T4 schema-map: 99 schemas, byRegistry portfolio=42 ectropy-domain=57.

decisions.title: luhtech-schema T5.4 — schema-map prefix ectropy-domain/ → ectropy/ + 99/99 D2 closure
decisions.context: T5.1 (Ectropy-ai/schemas#5) converted 57 Ectropy domain schemas from URN to URL $id form at https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/<path>, renamed 4 XaaS directories (caas→corsx, gaas→govsx, paas→paysx, maas→matsx), and established .roadmap/ in the Ectropy registry. T5.3 (Ectropy-Business#143) migrated consumer-side: 2 service-file URN→URL edits + 4 local-copy directory renames + 61 URN conversions across .roadmap/schemas/. T5.2 was correctly struck — @ectropy/schemas npm ships TypeScript types only; JSON schema directory changes did not affect the npm distribution surface. T5.4 closes the arc by aligning luhtech-schema schema_map.py with D-T5-A canonical hosting form.
decisions.decision: Update luhtech-schema schema_map.py path prefix from ectropy-domain/ to ectropy/ (matching D-T5-A canonical URL hosting at https://schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/<path>). Normalize byRegistry dict key from ectropy-domain to ectropy. Regenerate schema-map.json against post-T5.1 Ectropy registry (commit 2a9aee1). Result: 99/99 D2 compliance across both registers (42 portfolio + 57 Ectropy, all URL $id form). L11 SH-1 CLOSED.
decisions.rationale: The ectropy-domain/ prefix was a T4 placeholder selected before D-T5-A locked the canonical hosting URL. With T5.1 + T5.3 merged, the map path prefix must match the authoritative URL form so that byRegistry statistics, local-resolver coverage, and downstream tools reading paths from schema-map.json all use the canonical hosting path. Identity-mapping the dict key (ectropy-domain → ectropy) closes the same gap at the statistics-aggregation layer.

positive: L11 SH-1 CLOSED: 99/99 schemas D2-compliant across portfolio + Ectropy registries.
schema-map.json paths align with canonical hosting form (schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/<path>).
byRegistry key normalized — downstream consumers read a single canonical key.
T5 arc complete: T5.1 merged, T5.2 struck, T5.3 merged, T5.4 closed.
negative: Breaking change to byRegistry key for any consumer reading byRegistry['ectropy-domain']; must migrate to byRegistry['ectropy'].
risks: L11 SH-2 (54/57 Ectropy schemas missing top-level version) remains open — FU-L11-VERSION-FIELDS-1 in Ectropy registry tracks.
schema-map.schema.json version may warrant a major bump for the byRegistry key change; deferred.

alternatives.option: Keep the ectropy-domain/ prefix to maintain backward compatibility with T4 consumers.
alternatives.rejectionReason: D-T5-A locks schemas.luh.tech/ectropy/ as the single source of truth for L11 hosting. Permitting map-vs-host divergence creates a second canonical form and undermines schema-first discipline.
